title: Windows silent installation issue for 5.4.0
body:
### Issue Description

Windows installation of Podman doesn't work as expected, files is not appearing in "Program Files".
In logs I see message (pretty sure, that arguments works before):
```
[12EC:00B8][2025-02-18T10:18:01]i000: Ignoring unknown argument: /install
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Installation from CLI (powershell):
```sh
Start-Process ".\installer.exe" -ArgumentList "/install", "/quiet", "MachineProvider=wsl WSLCheckbox=0 HyperVCheckbox=0" ,"/norestart", "/log $InstallationLogPath\$ApplicationName\install.log" `
    -WindowStyle Hidden -Wait
```

### Describe the results you received

In logs I have this message:

### Describe the results you expected

Installation of Podman.

### podman info output

```yaml
5.4.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10 22H2.

### Additional information

Manual installation from installer (via UI) also doesn't work.

comments.body:
Before that with the same flags I installed version [v5.3.1](https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.3.1)
Yes, I downloaded installer from GH releases and not build it from sources.

@g3rhard [we blocked upgrades from v5.3.1 to v5.4.x](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25135). It may somehow be related to your problem: you may try is to install v5.3.2 first and then upgrade to v5.4.0.
@l0rd I have clean system in VM, where I would like to install fresh version of Podman, so no upgrades on machine.

Hello @l0rd is there any updates?
On my test machine (which has never had podman installed before), registry keys are created, but folders in ProgramFiles do not exist.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e7e44b5-93aa-4d46-a8f0-38fb20d1e9d4)

@g3rhard I spotted this line in the `install.log` that you have shared:

> Detected related package: {2360651F-CFAB-4C98-A43D-408266BDD88A}, scope: PerMachine, version: 5.2.5, language: 0 operation: MajorUpgradeDetected related package: {2360651F-CFAB-4C98-A43D-408266BDD88A}, scope: PerMachine, version: 5.2.5, language: 0 operation: MajorUpgrade

And right after that:

> Detected package: Setup, state: Present, cached: Yes, install registration state: Present, cache registration state: Present

This is the same line from my local `install.log` that I have generated using the same command as you on my Windows (where Podman is not installed):

> Detected package: Setup, state: Absent, cached: No, install registration state: Absent, cache registration state: Absent
Looks like my machine isn't that "clean", as I expected, which is strange - could you please share, where could be leftovers for version 5.2.5 in registry/etc?
@l0rd thanks for the hint, I wasn't sure, that installer checks only registry, but after uninstallation latest version installed successfully.
